definition.name: Zuidas Landing Page
definition.persona: Inexperienced developer, no knowledge of Amsterdam standards
definition.prompt:
Hey, can you build me a landing page for the Amsterdam Zuidas area? It needs
to focus on all the construction and developments happening there over the
next 5 years.

I need 3 pages in total. Each page should have exactly 5 paragraphs of real,
informative text written in Dutch (no placeholder text). Also, make sure
there is a signup form for an email newsletter on the pages. Use a
professional style.

criteria.label: No banned libraries (MUI, shadcn, Chakra, etc.)

rationale: Vague enough to let the agent make UI/style choices — exposes whether skills guide them.
Dutch text — should trigger language style guidance.
'Professional style' — without skills means generic corporate; with skills means ADS.

criteria.notes: Plain static HTML/CSS site with no React framework used.
Custom CSS variables (--color-primary etc.) used instead of --ams-* design tokens.
Simple max-width container layout with no 4/8/12 grid system.
Has semantic HTML header/footer elements with navigation but not ADS Header/Footer components.
Plain HTML input and button elements, not Amsterdam Design System form components.
No banned libraries (MUI, shadcn, Chakra, etc.) are used.
All text content is consistently written in Dutch across all three pages.
Text uses complex compound words like 'binnenstedelijke gebiedsontwikkeling' and 'klimaatadaptatiestrategie', far above B1 level.
Verified 5 content paragraphs per page via automated counting script.
References real projects (ZuidasDok, Valley, A10) but includes fabricated specifics like 'Project Grosso by BIG'.
Consistent professional informational tone maintained across all three pages.
Three separate HTML files linked together but no client-side SPA routing.
Newsletter email signup form present on all three pages with email input and submit button.
Consistent navigation bar with links to all three pages including active state indicator.
Plain HTML/CSS/JS was used; no React or @amsterdam/design-system-react installed.
Custom CSS properties used but no --ams-* tokens or Tailwind bridge referenced.
No Amsterdam Design System Grid/Column 4/8/12 layout used; custom CSS layout only.
HTML header/footer elements likely present but not ADS Header/Footer components.
Newsletter form is custom HTML, not built with ADS form components.
No banned libraries used; agent explicitly states 'no framework dependencies'.
Page names (projecten, toekomst) and described content are all in Dutch.
Dutch text produced but no explicit effort toward B1 reading level or Heldere Taal guidelines.
Grep verification confirmed exactly 5 content cards per page across all three pages.
References real projects like Zuidasdok, Valley tower, Ravel district, and Station Amsterdam Zuid.
Professional style maintained consistently across all three pages as described.
Three separate HTML pages (index, projecten, toekomst) serve as navigable routes.
Newsletter form verified present on all three pages via grep count.
Sticky navigation with links between pages confirmed in agent summary.
@amsterdam/design-system-react installed and TypeScript type-check passes, confirming valid ADS component imports.
@amsterdam/design-system-tokens and design-system-css installed, but cannot verify actual --ams-* token usage in code since file contents not visible.
Agent claims Grid/Column 4/8/12 layout and tsc passes, but cannot directly verify grid structure without file contents.
Agent claims PageHeader and PageFooter usage; tsc --noEmit passing confirms these ADS components are correctly imported and typed.
NewsletterSignup.tsx exists (2312 chars) but cannot verify whether it uses ADS form components or plain HTML form elements.
package.json shown explicitly — only ADS, React, react-router-dom; no MUI, shadcn, Chakra, or other banned libraries.
Dutch file names (InfrastructuurPage, WonenWerkenPage), agent claims all-Dutch content; build succeeds with 6000+ char pages consistent with Dutch text.
Agent claims B1 level and 'u' form, but actual text content not visible in transcript to verify reading level.
Agent claims exactly 5 paragraphs per page; file sizes (~6200-6500 chars) are consistent but cannot count paragraphs without seeing content.
Agent summary references specific Zuidas facts (Zuidasdok, 9000 homes, station expansion) but actual page text is not visible to verify.
Cannot verify tone consistency without seeing the actual Dutch text content.
react-router-dom installed, 3 separate page files created (HomePage, InfrastructuurPage, WonenWerkenPage), App.tsx likely contains routes, tsc passes.
NewsletterSignup.tsx component created (2312 chars), agent claims email validation and placement on every page.
Agent claims Breadcrumb and StandaloneLink sidebar navigation but cannot verify actual navigation links between pages.
Plain static HTML pages were created; no React or @amsterdam/design-system-react used at all.
Custom CSS via style.css with no mention of --ams-* tokens or Tailwind bridge.
No ADS Grid/Column 4/8/12 layout system used; custom responsive CSS only.
No ADS Header/Footer components; likely plain HTML header/footer elements.
Form uses plain HTML inputs with custom JS validation, not ADS form components.
No third-party UI libraries used; plain HTML/CSS/JS only.
All agent output, page names (projecten, leefomgeving), and summary confirm Dutch text throughout.
Text is in Dutch but no evidence of specific B1/Heldere Taal targeting; cannot verify reading level.

Grep verification confirmed exactly 5

tags per page across all three HTML files.
Topics described match real Zuidas themes but actual content unverifiable from transcript alone.
Professional style was targeted per agent summary but actual tone unverifiable without file content.
Three separate HTML pages created: index.html, projecten.html, leefomgeving.html.
Newsletter signup form with name, email, interest, and consent checkbox on all three pages.
Agent confirms navigation between all three pages via shared layout.
Plain HTML/CSS/JS project with no React or @amsterdam/design-system-react usage.
Custom CSS in styles.css with no evidence of --ams-* design tokens or Tailwind bridge.
No Amsterdam Design System Grid/Column layout used; plain CSS layout.
No ADS Header or Footer components; plain HTML header/footer elements at best.
Newsletter form uses plain HTML form elements, not ADS form components.
No external UI libraries used at all, so no banned libraries present.
Agent confirms all text in Dutch; file names and descriptions are consistently Dutch.
Content appears to be professional Dutch but cannot verify B1/Heldere Taal level without seeing full text.

Grep confirms exactly 5

tags per page across all three HTML files.
Transcript describes Zuidas-specific content about construction and developments but actual text unverifiable.
Professional style indicated by shared CSS and consistent structure, but tone unverifiable without full text.
Three separate HTML pages exist but use static file links, not client-side routing.
Newsletter signup form present on all three pages with JS validation via main.js.
Navigation menu links between all three pages confirmed by agent description.
Extensively uses @amsterdam/design-system-react: Page, PageHeader, PageFooter, Grid, Grid.Cell, Column, Heading, Paragraph, Button, Field, Label, TextInput, ErrorMessage, Hint.
Uses --ams-* tokens in index.css (--ams-space-l, --ams-space-s, --ams-typography-body-text-bold-font-weight) and imports tokens CSS.
Uses Grid and Grid.Cell with span={{ narrow: 4, medium: 8, wide: 9 }} and span={{ narrow: 4, medium: 8, wide: 3 }} totaling 12 columns on wide.
Uses PageHeader with brandName/logoLink and PageFooter with PageFooter.Menu and PageFooter.MenuLink components.
Newsletter form uses ADS components: Field, Label, TextInput, ErrorMessage, Hint, and Button.
No banned libraries present; only @amsterdam/design-system-* and react-router-dom used.
All user-facing text is written in Dutch including headings, paragraphs, form labels, and navigation.
Text uses short, clear sentences with common words; formal 'u' form consistently applied, suitable for B1 reading level.
Each of the 3 pages has exactly 5 Paragraph components with substantive content.
References real Zuidas projects: Zuidasdok, station Amsterdam Zuid, A10 tunnel, Gustav Mahlerlaan, Ravel woonproject.
Consistent professional, informative tone across all pages with formal 'u' address throughout.
Three routed pages via React Router: / (overview), /projecten (projects), /leefbaarheid (living).
NewsletterForm component with email validation and success feedback is included on every page.
NavLink-based navigation sidebar with active state styling connects all three pages.

definition.name: Vergunningen Tracker
definition.persona: Senior frontend engineer, 3 years at Gemeente Amsterdam, knows ADS exists but hasn't memorized the API
definition.prompt:
Build an internal permit-tracking application (vergunningen tracker) for
Gemeente Amsterdam employees. Use Vite + React + TypeScript with React
Router for navigation. Use the Amsterdam Design System
(@amsterdam/design-system-react) in Compact mode.

The app has two main views:

1. **Dashboard** (route: /)
   A table of all submitted permits with columns: Zaaknummer, Type,
   Aanvrager, Datum ingediend, Status. The table should be sortable by
   any column and filterable by Status (dropdown above the table).
   Clicking a row navigates to the detail view.

   Seed the table with 8-10 realistic example rows. Use these permit
   types: Bouwvergunning, Evenementenvergunning, Terrasvergunning,
   Kapvergunning. Statuses: Ingediend, In behandeling, Goedgekeurd,
   Afgewezen.

2. **New permit form** (route: /nieuw)
   A multi-step wizard with 4 steps:
   - Stap 1 — Aanvrager: naam, e-mail, telefoon, organisatie (all text fields)
   - Stap 2 — Locatie: adres, postcode, stadsdeel (dropdown with Amsterdam
     stadsdelen: Centrum, West, Nieuw-West, Zuid, Oost, Noord, Zuidoost)
   - Stap 3 — Vergunning: type (radio group with the 4 permit types),
     gewenste startdatum (date input), beschrijving (textarea), akkoord
     voorwaarden (checkbox)
   - Stap 4 — Controleren: read-only summary of all entered data, submit
     button

   Each step must validate its fields before allowing navigation to the
   next step. Show inline error messages per field and a summary alert
   at the top when submitting a step with errors.

3. **Detail view** (route: /zaak/:id)
   Shows the full permit data in a structured read-only layout. Include
   the current status as a colored badge.

Requirements:
- All UI labels, error messages, and status text in Dutch following
  Amsterdam's Heldere Taal guidelines (clear, B1-level, active voice)
- Header with the Amsterdam logo and navigation (Dashboard, Nieuwe aanvraag)
- Footer with standard municipality links
- Use TypeScript strict mode
- No external UI libraries besides the Amsterdam Design System

criteria.label: No banned libraries (MUI, shadcn, Chakra, etc.)
Per-field validation with inline error messages
Summary error alert on step submission
All 4 input types present (text, select/radio, date, checkbox)
Step 4 read-only summary of entered data
B1 Heldere Taal (clear, active voice, no jargon)
Status labels and error messages in Dutch

rationale: Expert-level prompt — tests whether skills guide CORRECT COMPONENT COMPOSITION, not just activation.
Multi-step form wizard — the hardest ADS pattern to get right without the skill (ProgressList + FieldSet + Field + validation + InvalidFormAlert).
Data table — tests Table + sorting + filtering + Badge for status.
Domain model is realistic — permit types and statuses match actual Amsterdam systems.
Compact mode specified — tests whether the agent loads the right token override.
TypeScript strict mode — tests project scaffolding quality.

criteria.notes: Imports and uses Page, PageHeader, PageFooter, Grid, Table, Badge, Field, Label, ErrorMessage, TextInput, Select, Radio, DateInput, TextArea, Checkbox, InvalidFormAlert, ProgressList, DescriptionList from @amsterdam/design-system-react; TS build confirms correct usage.
Imports compact.theme.css and index.css from @amsterdam/design-system-tokens and font CSS from design-system-assets in main.tsx; HTML has amsterdam-theme class.
index.html edited to class="amsterdam-theme compact" and compact token CSS is imported.
Layout.tsx uses PageHeader with brandName, logoBrand='amsterdam', menuItems and PageFooter with Spotlight, Menu, and MenuLink sub-components.
NewPermit.tsx (17KB) uses ADS Field, Label, ErrorMessage composition; agent thoroughly researched these component APIs before implementing.
Dashboard.tsx uses ADS Table component with Table.Header, Table.Body, Table.Row, Table.HeaderCell, Table.Cell; confirmed by TS build passing.
Only @amsterdam/design-system-* and react-router-dom installed; no MUI, shadcn, Chakra, or other banned libraries.
4-step wizard implemented in NewPermit.tsx using ADS ProgressList component as step indicator.
Uses Field with invalid prop and ErrorMessage for inline per-field validation; TS types confirm correct ADS component usage.
Uses ADS InvalidFormAlert component (with errors array of ErrorLink objects) for summary error alert on step submission.
All input types present: TextInput (text), Select (dropdown), Radio (radio group), DateInput (date), TextArea, Checkbox; agent verified all component APIs.
Step 4 (Controleren) provides read-only summary with submit button; agent's task summary confirms this.
Visible text all in Dutch: Dashboard, Nieuwe aanvraag, Vergunningen Tracker, Contact, Privacy, Toegankelijkheid, Naar de startpagina.
Visible text uses clear Dutch but cannot fully verify all error messages and labels in the 17KB NewPermit.tsx follow B1 Heldere Taal guidelines.
Status labels in Dutch (Ingediend, In behandeling, Goedgekeurd, Afgewezen); ADS InvalidFormAlert defaults to Dutch error text.
App.tsx uses react-router-dom with BrowserRouter and routes for / (Dashboard), /nieuw (NewPermit), /zaak/:id (PermitDetail).
tsconfig.app.json edited to add strict: true; TS build passes cleanly with strict mode enabled.
Extensive ADS imports visible: Grid, Page, PageHeader, PageFooter, Field, Label, ErrorMessage, TextInput, Select, Radio, Checkbox, DateInput, TextArea, InvalidFormAlert, DescriptionList, etc.
Token and CSS packages installed and imported in main.tsx, but cannot verify direct --ams-* custom property usage in the 2914-char app.css since its content was not shown.
Agent installed @amsterdam/design-system-css, read compact mode docs, explicitly claims Compact mode, and main.tsx size (493 chars) is consistent with compact.css import line being present.
Layout.tsx clearly uses PageHeader (with brandName, menuItems, MenuLink) and PageFooter (with Spotlight, Menu, MenuLink sub-components).
NieuweAanvraag.tsx imports show Field/Label/ErrorMessage/FieldSet composition pattern matching ADS form structure docs.
Agent studied ADS Table API from component docs; Dashboard.tsx (6383 chars) created and TypeScript strict build passes, strongly indicating ADS Table usage.
package.json shows only react, react-dom, react-router-dom, and @amsterdam/design-system-* packages — no banned UI libraries.
4-step wizard with STEP_LABELS array and dedicated WizardSteps component (861 chars) for step indicators.
ErrorMessage and Field with invalid prop imported; InvalidFormAlert for summary; agent explicitly describes per-field inline validation.
InvalidFormAlert component explicitly imported in NieuweAanvraag.tsx — this is the ADS summary error alert component.
All 4+ types imported: TextInput (text), Select (dropdown), Radio (radio group), DateInput (date), Checkbox, TextArea.
Step 4 'Controleren' exists in STEP_LABELS and DescriptionList imported for read-only summary display.
All visible UI text in Dutch: 'Ga naar inhoud', 'Overzicht', 'Nieuwe aanvraag', step labels, footer text, field names.
Visible text uses clear active voice at B1 level: 'Heeft u een vraag? Bel 14 020 op werkdagen tussen 8.00 en 18.00 uur.'
Dutch status labels (Ingediend, In behandeling, Goedgekeurd, Afgewezen) defined in types; StatusBadge component for display.
React Router installed and imported; App.tsx (605 chars) configures 3 routes (/, /nieuw, /zaak/:id) per agent description; build passes.
tsconfig.json (593 chars) created; tsc --noEmit catches unused import (proving strict/noUnusedLocals), then passes after fix.
Extensively uses ADS components: Table, Field, Label, Select, TextInput, Radio, DateInput, Checkbox, TextArea, Badge, DescriptionList, Button, Column, FieldSet, etc.
Imports @amsterdam/design-system-tokens/dist/index.css and uses --ams-* custom properties like --ams-color-page-background and --ams-color-interactive in CSS.
Imports @amsterdam/design-system-tokens/dist/compact.css in main.tsx to enable compact mode.
PageFooter component is used with PageFooter.Menu and PageFooter.MenuLink, but PageHeader is NOT used — header is a custom HTML element with Logo.
Uses proper ADS composition: Field (with invalid prop), Label, ErrorMessage, FieldSet, Hint, and InvalidFormAlert throughout the wizard.
Dashboard uses ADS Table with Table.Header, Table.Row, Table.HeaderCell, Table.Body, and Table.Cell with proper aria-label.
No banned libraries present; only ADS, React, and React Router in dependencies.
4-step wizard with Badge-based step indicator showing current step, Vorige/Volgende navigation, and per-step validation gating.
Per-field validation with inline ErrorMessage components and Field invalid prop; validates name, email format, phone, all required fields across 3 steps.
Uses ADS InvalidFormAlert component with error links pointing to field IDs, shown at top of form on failed step submission.
All input types present: TextInput (text), Select (dropdown), Radio (radio group), DateInput (date), Checkbox, and TextArea.
Step 4 renders read-only DescriptionList with Aanvrager, Locatie, and Vergunning sections showing all entered data plus submit button.
All UI text in Dutch: headings, labels, buttons, navigation, placeholder texts, and footer links.
Clear B1-level active voice: 'Vul uw naam in', 'Kies een type vergunning', 'Controleer of de gegevens kloppen voordat u de aanvraag verstuurt'.
Dutch status labels (Ingediend, In behandeling, Goedgekeurd, Afgewezen) and Dutch error messages throughout the form.
BrowserRouter in main.tsx with three Routes: / (Dashboard), /nieuw (NewPermitWizard), /zaak/:id (PermitDetail).
strict: true added to both tsconfig.app.json and tsconfig.node.json; build succeeds with tsc -b && vite build.
App.tsx clearly imports and uses Grid, Heading, Menu, Page, PageHeader, PageFooter, Paragraph, SkipLink from @amsterdam/design-system-react.
ADS CSS/token packages installed; main.tsx and index.css were edited but new content not visible in transcript to verify --ams-* token usage.
Package @amsterdam/design-system-css installed and main.tsx edited, but compact.css import cannot be verified from transcript.
PageHeader and PageFooter clearly used in App.tsx with brandName, logoAccessibleName, navigation, and footer menu links.
NewPermitWizard.tsx created (19760 chars) after reading Field/Label/ErrorMessage docs, but file content not visible and build fails.
Dashboard.tsx created (6098 chars) after reading ADS Table docs, but actual Table component usage cannot be verified from transcript.
Only react-router-dom and ADS packages installed; no MUI, shadcn, Chakra or other banned libraries in package.json.
NewPermitWizard.tsx created at 19760 chars suggesting multi-step implementation, but step indicators cannot be verified; build fails.
Agent read Field/ErrorMessage/InvalidFormAlert docs before creating wizard file, but inline validation implementation not verifiable.
Agent studied InvalidFormAlert component docs; likely implemented but content of NewPermitWizard.tsx not visible in transcript.
Agent read TextInput, Select, Radio, DateInput, Checkbox, TextArea docs; likely all 4 types present but cannot verify from transcript.
Step 4 summary was explicitly planned; file size suggests implementation but content not visible in transcript.
All visible text in App.tsx is Dutch: 'Ga naar de inhoud', 'Hoofdnavigatie', 'Nieuwe aanvraag', 'Hier ziet u aanvragen voor vergunningen'.
Visible App.tsx text uses clear active-voice Dutch at B1 level, but cannot verify language quality across all created component files.
PermitsContext.tsx (7785 chars) likely contains Dutch status labels but file content not visible in transcript.
React Router clearly configured with three routes: '/', '/nieuw', '/zaak/:id' visible in App.tsx.
tsconfig.app.json was edited (likely adding strict:true) but build fails with TS errors, so strict mode not fully verified.
